Hackers can only get away with this kind of stuff on ETH because ETH doesn't have a good governance. The funds of EVERY exploit on DOT were recovered. We can't say that for ETH or for any other ecosystem.

It's laughable how people try to portray DOT as getting hacked when in reality the exploit happened on ETH, not DOT.

Look at the picture this guy posted >>62110117. Why are the looking at the exploit on etherscan, if it's a DOT exploit? How come we see "Transfer 108 ETH from Uniswap V4: Pool..."? Do you notice what I mean? The exploit is on ETH, not DOT. And even if the exploit happened on DOT governance would freeze the funds and revert the transactions.

Another thing. The exploit happened on Hyperbridge, on a smart contract, on the side of Ethereum. And Hyperbridge is a corechain on DOT. Do you notice how we're basically 2 hops away from DOT?. Hyperbridge is a corechain on DOT (1st hop). Hyperbridge has smart contracts deployed on ETH in order for them to bridge between Hyperbridge and ETH (2nd hop). The exploit was an ETH smart contract exploit on Ethereum. If the same exploit happened on the side of Hyperbridge then the funds could EASILY be recovered through either the Hyperbridge governance (which is a copy of DOTs gov system. That's the whole point. That the corechains inherit DOTs functionality, therefore it's superior governance system) or the Polkadot governance.

He got the admin right to the contract, therefore he could do an infinite mint

Open thread • Replies: 72 • Updated: 2026-04-14 13:46 UTC

OP

I love watching normie carhag/carfag compilations where Normies complain about how everything is too expensive.

I live on ~24k a year in a HCOL city making $26/hr
>$820 rent utilities included
>free transportation (fare fags aren’t checking train during my commute hours)
>$300/mo groceries (steak and potatoes mostly)
>$60/mo unlimited data
>$15/mo rent insurance
>$30 dental insurance *shrugs*

I regularly spend $8 on coffee, $15 on cigs, $30 dollars on pizza, and I STILL usually dump minimum $1500 a month into my brokerage account. Next month my credit limit will go from 12k>24k when I open another chase CC. I have 6 months expenses in a savings account and the equivalent in dry powder in my brokerage.

Being completely unstressed/concerned about money and whipping out my girthy 12k CL sapphire preferred card on cutie coffee sluts 4 times a week to order my $9 dollar 24oz drum of double chocolate mocha and then ripping a Newport 100 on a comfy bench while i enjoy my coffee is all the pleasure I need in this world

>>62114560
First, the “it was before the current draft” excuse doesn’t really hold up. The core direction of the Clarity Act hasn’t magically flipped overnight, the same underlying concerns about market structure, jurisdiction and industry impact were already part of the discussion back then. Acting like his comments are completely irrelevant now is a complete stretch lol

Second, saying he “helped form it” and is now doing a media tour supporting it doesn’t prove consistency, if anything, it raises the opposite question: why the shift? Either:

A) he was criticizing something he didn’t fully understand at the time
or
B) his stance is adapting to what benefits Ripple in the moment

Neither of those is exactly a strong defense. Brad is clearly a grifter, flip flops on opinions when it suits him best. He doesn't know what he wants.

OP

Back in my day we didn't sit around waiting for some cartoon frog coin to make us rich. We got off our asses, worked 50-60 hours a week, saved every dime we could, and bought real estate like actual men. Bricks and mortar. Dirt you can stand on. Something that puts a roof over your head and cash in your pocket every month from rent. Not this digital tulip mania.Stop gambling your life savings on vaporware and start building something real. Get a real job, stop buying $8 coffees, and put your money into property that actually exists. That's how you build wealth, son. Not by staring at green candles all day.

all these kids glued to charts like it’s a full time job, watching magic internet coins go up and down like it means something. no skills, no assets, just memes and cope. dopamine hits every time a line goes green, panic the second it dips.

meanwhile you ask them what they actually own and it’s nothing. no land, no trade, no long term plan, just screenshots and hopium.

maybe i really was born in the wrong era. back when investing meant actually building something instead of praying your jpeg or frog token moons overnight. you worked, you saved, you bought something real, and you held it. simple.
now it’s bro trust the plan and this one’s different every six months. same cycle, different buzzwords.

real estate, steady work, owning something tangible, that’s real wealth. not refreshing an app at 2am hoping for green candles.

call me old fashioned but i’d rather have keys in my hand than pixels on a screen.

item.title: >deflation is le bad — 10 replies — 35.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>deflation is le bad

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-04-14 13:54 UTC

OP

>but if deflation is bad how come pple is the most valuable company when phone prices have deflated 50% over 25 years?
>it just is, okay
Why hasn't TV-manufacturers gone out of business? There's no way a deflationary economic model is long term sustainable, is there?
Even the price of software has gone down 75%. Why is Microsoft still valued so high?

Early replies (first 3)

62113176
>>62113170
Trump made everything more expensive

62113177
>>62113170
apple phone prices go up every year.

62113208
>>62113177
The way they measure inflation for phones is retarded. You think: it’s just the price, right?

No. They measure bullshit like “new features” like a barely upgraded camera and argue that: “well, it’s not really the same phone. It’s improved. Yes it’s gone up in price but the features are actually enhancing the phone well beyond what that price increase suggests. Thus, it’s really deflating if you think about it.” So whenever apple does literally anything to change their phone, the inflation is reduced despite the price increasing.

Not even making it up. It’s fucking retarded, and OP is retarded for using cellphones as an example of deflation. This is also by design. You know why inflation feels way higher than 3.2%? Goodhart put it well when he argued that a metric becomes useless once it becomes the target because, in that event, everyone fucks with the data to make the target. The way in which CPI is calculated is nonsensical on purpose. Don’t even get me started on how they assess housing…

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62113212
>>62113208
Yep. And if something gets worse they ignore it.

62114825
Deflation doesn't exist, because if prices go down, consumer spending power goes up, which makes the economy good, and causes inflation. Plus government will freak out and print a shit load of money so there's always an upwards bias.

"People will just hoard money"

They fuckin' won't. It burns a hole in their pocket.

62114857
>dumb /biz/ poster doesn't understand that people will just buy more phone if it's cheaper

62114872
>>62113170
This chart is deceptive because the value for tech products are normalized by computing power/storage etc. If a new 16GB phone is the same price as an old 8GB phone, they count that as 50% deflation. This completely ignores that modern apps are bloated as ever and will takeup whatever computing or storage capacity the mainstream phone allows.

62114888
The things on top have actually doubled / tripled in price and their original price was 5 / 6 figs.
The stuff on the bottom costs like a couple hundred bucks maybe if you can't find them for free at the store, basically the only thing we're in a "golden age" for is clothing and couches.

I keep going to estate sales and finding cashmere, angora, lamb's wool, ect. for like 10 bucks, all good quality pieces.

62114894
>>62113170
TV manufacturers make money off of analytics now. Get with the times, gramps.

62115016
>>62113170
You're confusing revenue with profit

62084718
Funny of you to ask OP, I remember my struggles of landing my first WFH job. Btw, WFH stands for Work from Home because not many people come across that term usually. But, if you must ask, our company is headquartered in Nashville. My base pay is 95k per year along with a COL stipend and generous health and pension benefits. As we speak, I am clocked into my position in the beautiful hot springs in British Columbia. We do have an RTO policy, but I haven't been to the office in 4 years! Ha-Ha! You COULDN'T pay me to work in the office. Yesterday I had fun fucking a uni student that transferred from china for the school years, so yeah life is great so far. Today she is making me her traditional Chinese dinner as a thank you for fucking her so nicely. Maybe next month I'll fly out to Holland and do my job for the month. But like I said OP, keep your chin up and don't be disuaded by the multiple rejection emails in your future until you get that golden offer. Take it and run with it. Anyways .... Chiao!

item.title: A sincerely serious question for experienced /biz/nessman — 73 replies — 9.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
A sincerely serious question for experienced /biz/nessman

Open thread • Replies: 73 • Updated: 2026-04-14 06:40 UTC

OP

Can someone explain this shit to me? I know that stock market != real economy, and I know that market can remain irrational longer than I can remain solvent yadadada. But isn't it in theory, supposed to at least loosely map to fundamentals and signals?
There is no end to war.
The ceasefire is tentative and unlikely to hold.
Even if we miraculously reach to a conclusion soon:
The disruptions and shortages will last for many months.
A significant chunk of world's energy infrastructure is destroyed and will take years to rebuild.
Shortage of fuel and its inflationary effects will continue for the rest of the year at minimum.
Fertilizer prices will inevitably cause food inflation during the next harvest.
Further shortages and disruptions of aluminum and helium will affect many industries.
And yet the market is incredibly calm and constantly rallying behind even the most obvious "damage control for the markets" news. Or hell even to the most negative news. The DOW is almost back to where it was late February. Why is this the case? The only explanations I can think of are:
We are in the middle of what would otherwise have been a legendary bull run and even the colossal fuck ups in the world stage can't fully blunt it. It's simply absorbing every negative signal and keep pushing forwards with incredible momentum, only slowed down a bit.
The economy, at this point is under total control of a handful of massive institutional investors like Blackrock and other funds. This historically unprecedented consolidation turned it into a fake gay circus. They call the shots, every other player is liquidity fodder for them. And they are unwilling to call the bottom yet, so the show goes on.
There is no conspiracy. Market is remaining irrational for a prolonged period for some reason, but eventually it will react appropriately to current shortages and price accordingly.
What else could be? What do you think? Help this retard understand.

Early replies (first 3)

62112281
AI is fucking here. It is that simple

62112285
>>62112276
who cares nerd, line goes up

62112299
The market runs on fake news headlines now. Perception is reality nigga and reality is looking pretty good! Iran's military no longer exists and now they have no economy. That's what matters. Gas prices, unemployment, and the other real world issues you are worried about only affect the goyim.

62114131

speculate

1
a: to meditate on or ponder a subject : reflect
b: to review something idly or casually and often inconclusively
2: to assume a business risk in hope of gain
especially : to make a relatively risky investment in something (such as stocks or real estate) in the hope of making a large short-term profit from market fluctuations

transitive verb

>>>>>1: to take to be true on the basis of insufficient evidence : theorize
2: to be curious or doubtful about : wonder
speculates whether it will rain all vacation
